Title: Elucidation of the pathway for biosynthesis of saponin adjuvants from the soapbark tree

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression; promoter type NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark; also detected in leaves, primordia, stems, roots of Q. saponaria
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds glucuronic acid to quillaic acid at C-3 to form QA-Mono, enabling initiation of the C-3 sugar chain.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression with the four QA pathway genes in N. benthamiana converted QA to a more polar product. 2. NMR confirmed the product as 3-O-β-D-glucopyranosiduronic acid-QA (QA-Mono). 3. CSLM1 therefore functions as a small-molecule glucuronosyltransferase initiating C-3 glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; large-scale infiltration; purification; 1D/2D NMR structural confirmation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like M (CSL-M) family glycosyltransferase-like protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in primordial tissue at moderate levels; not coexpressed with QsbAS1 (PCC -0.59)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; coexpression with tHMGR to boost triterpene yield
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although functional in heterologous expression, CSLM2 showed much stronger coexpression with pathway genes and was suggested to be the primary in planta enzyme.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing the early C-3 glycosylation module in heterologous QS saponin engineering.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq4000) for six Q. saponaria tissues; genome assembly using PacBio Sequel and Hi-C

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression; promoter type NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark; also detected in leaves, primordia, stems, roots of Q. saponaria
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds glucuronic acid to quillaic acid at C-3 to form QA-Mono, initiating the C-3 oligosaccharide branch.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression with QA biosynthetic genes generated QA-Mono from QA. 2. Product identity was confirmed as 3-O-β-D-glucopyranosiduronic acid-QA. 3. Strong coexpression with QsbAS1 suggests CSLM2 is likely the primary QA glucuronosyltransferase in Q. saponaria.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; purification; 1D/2D NMR
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like M (CSL-M) family glycosyltransferase-like protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very highly coexpressed with QsbAS1; strongest expression in leaf primordia
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; coexpression with tHMGR to boost triterpene yield
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: An unusual example of a CSL protein acting on a small-molecule triterpene scaffold rather than canonical cell wall polysaccharide synthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key entry enzyme for engineering QS saponin glycosylation in heterologous hosts.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq4000) for six Q. saponaria tissues; genome assembly using PacBio Sequel and Hi-C

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: QS saponins containing D-fucose, including QS-7 pathway intermediates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark; also other tissues contain QS-21
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Greatly boosts D-fucosylated C-28 glycoside formation by reducing the transferred 4-keto-6-deoxy-D-glucose moiety on the saponin scaffold to D-fucose in situ.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression substantially increased QA-TriX-F and downstream C-28 glycosides in N. benthamiana. 2. In vitro assays showed it does not produce UDP-D-fucose directly from UDP-4-keto-6-deoxy-D-glucose. 3. Instead, it reduces scaffold-bound 4-keto-6-deoxy-D-glucose after transfer by UGT74BX1, establishing a new plant route to D-fucosylation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression; LC-MS; recombinant protein purification; in vitro enzyme assays; heat-inactivation stepwise assay; NMR sugar nucleotide analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with QsbAS1 (PCC 0.871); similar expression pattern to QS pathway genes in primordia
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro reactions with ATCV-1 UGD and UGT74BX1
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene was renamed QsFucSyn. It defines a previously unknown biosynthetic logic in plants: D-fucose is formed after sugar transfer to the aglycone rather than as a preformed nucleotide sugar.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enabling step for efficient production of D-fucosylated adjuvant saponins in heterologous systems; likely essential for engineering QS-7/QS-21-related molecules.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq4000); genome assembly using PacBio Sequel and Hi-C

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark; also detected across tissues for related QS saponins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts formation of the apiosylated C-28 product by supplying UDP-apiose/UDP-xylose, especially increasing the Qs0234140-dependent product.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QsAXS is highly expressed in leaf primordia and strongly coexpressed with QS genes. 2. Coexpression with terminal pentosyltransferases caused an ~11-fold increase in the Qs0234140 product. 3. This supports a role as UDP-D-apiose/UDP-D-xylose synthase supplying donor sugar for late C-28 glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS product quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaf primordia; strongly coexpressed with QsbAS1 (PCC 0.943)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not physically clustered with the main saponin genes but functionally important for overcoming presumed UDP-apiose limitation in N. benthamiana.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important accessory gene for improving yield of apiosylated QS scaffold intermediates during pathway reconstruction.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq4000); genome assembly using PacBio Sequel and Hi-C

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark (highest); also other tissues contain QS saponins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acetylates the C-28 sugar chain intermediate required for QS-7 biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening of BAHD acyltransferases identified Qs0206480 as the only candidate adding an acetyl group to advanced heptasaccharide scaffold 18. 2. Inclusion of this enzyme was required for production of QS-7 in tobacco. 3. The data support acetylation as a late essential step preceding terminal rhamnosylation in QS-7 formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening by transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; omission tests in full QS-7 reconstitution
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on chromosome 13 and not clustered with previously characterized QS genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential late tailoring enzyme for reconstructing QS-7 biosynthesis and engineering low-toxicity adjuvant saponins.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq4000); genome assembly using PacBio Sequel and Hi-C

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the core triterpene precursor for quillaic acid and all downstream QS saponins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed scaffold-forming step; key entry point
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark; also present in leaves and other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables β-amyrin production, initiating QS scaffold biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A single Q. saponaria candidate identified by BLAST against a known β-amyrin synthase produced β-amyrin in N. benthamiana. 2. GC-MS matched an authentic β-amyrin standard. 3. QsbAS1 was used as the foundational entry enzyme for reconstruction of the QS pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning from leaf cDNA; transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS against authentic standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed and coexpressed with downstream pathway genes; strongest in leaf primordia, lowest in old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; tHMGR coexpression used in production assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1KP transcriptome mining; RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq4000); genome assembly using PacBio Sequel and Hi-C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential starting enzyme for heterologous production of QS saponin scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified using licorice GgbAS1 as BLAST query.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes β-amyrin at C-28 to form oleanolic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an oxidized scaffold precursor to quillaic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key scaffold oxidation step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark; leaves and other tissues contain QS-21
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts β-amyrin almost completely to oleanolic acid in transient assays.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression with QsbAS1 in N. benthamiana resulted in near total conversion of β-amyrin to oleanolic acid. 2. It provides the C-28 oxidation required for quillaic acid biosynthesis. 3. In combination with CYP716A297 and CYP714E52, it enables full QA production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/GC-MS comparison with authentic standards
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed with QsbAS1; strongest in primordia
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential oxidation module for building QA scaffold in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified using Medicago truncatula CYP716A12 as query.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces C-16α oxidation in combination with C-28 oxidized scaffold formation to generate echinocystic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an advanced direct precursor to quillaic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key scaffold oxidation step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds C-16α oxidation needed for QA scaffold completion.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Alone it had little activity toward β-amyrin. 2. With QsbAS1 and CYP716A224 it produced echinocystic acid. 3. It is one of the three CYPs required to complete quillaic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient combinatorial expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/GC-MS with authentic standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed with QsbAS1; located adjacent to biosynthetic gene cluster 45; strongest in primordia
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required oxidation component for full QA pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Physically close to a saccharide-related biosynthetic gene cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Echinocystic acid and/or oleanolic acid-derived substrate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the scaffold at C-23 to complete formation of quillaic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the quillaic acid scaffold used for all downstream QS glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bark; also leaves and other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes the oxidized QA scaffold required for downstream glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening of candidate CYPs identified CYP714E52 as the missing oxidase needed for QA formation. 2. Coexpression with QsbAS1, CYP716A224, and CYP716A297 produced QA in N. benthamiana. 3. Structure of QA was confirmed by NMR after scale-up.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate CYP screen; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; purification; 1H NMR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed with QsbAS1; strongest in primordia
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for direct synthesis of QA scaffold in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also active on oleanolic acid, likely generating the C-23 aldehyde gypsogenin.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose to the C-3 glucuronoside of QA in β-1,2 linkage.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the disaccharide precursor required for branch-specific third-sugar addition at C-3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the second sugar of the C-3 chain, converting QA-Mono to QA-Di.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression with QA pathway genes and CSLM1 converted QA-Mono to a new hexose-containing product. 2. NMR identified the product as QA-Di with β-D-galactose added to the glucuronic acid. 3. UGT73CU3 therefore encodes the C-3 β-1,2-galactosyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; purification; 2D NMR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ranked third by coexpression with QsbAS1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for assembling the conserved C-3 disaccharide module of QS scaffolds.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose to QA-Di to form the C-3 trisaccharide QA-TriX.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms one of the two alternative C-3 trisaccharide branches found in QS saponins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: QS saponin intermediates and QS congeners with C-3 xylose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directs formation of the xylosylated C-3 trisaccharide branch.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression with the QA-Di-producing gene set generated a pentose-containing product. 2. NMR confirmed the product as QA-TriX. 3. UGT73CX1 is the xylosyltransferase responsible for one branch of C-3 trisaccharide diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; purification; 2D NMR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ranked sixth by coexpression with QsbAS1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for selecting the C-3 xylose branch during pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Alternative to UGT73CX2 rhamnosylation at the same branch position.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to QA-Di to form the C-3 trisaccharide QA-TriR.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the alternative C-3 trisaccharide branch used in many downstream pathway reconstructions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: QS saponin intermediates and congeners with C-3 rhamnose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directs formation of the rhamnosylated C-3 trisaccharide branch.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression with the QA-Di-producing gene set generated a deoxyhexose-containing product. 2. NMR confirmed the product as QA-TriR. 3. UGT73CX2 is the rhamnosyltransferase responsible for the alternate C-3 trisaccharide branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; purification; 2D NMR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables engineering of the C-3 rhamnose branch, which was preferred for purification and downstream C-28 studies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gave approximately twofold higher yields than the C-3 xylose branch in the heterologous system.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers UDP-4-keto-6-deoxy-D-glucose to the C-28 carboxyl through an ester linkage; the attached sugar is then reduced in situ to D-fucose by QsFucSyn.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Initiates the conserved C-28 tetrasaccharide chain required for QS-7/QS-21-type saponins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major bottleneck and key control point in heterologous production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: D-fucosylated QS saponins including QS-7 pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates C-28 glycosylation but shows poor conversion in N. benthamiana unless QsFucSyn is coexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the highest coexpressed UGT and only subgroup L candidate, consistent with ester-forming activity. 2. Coexpression formed low amounts of the putative C-28 fucosylated product. 3. In vitro work showed the enzyme likely uses UDP-4-keto-6-deoxy-D-glucose rather than UDP-D-fucose, redefining the mechanism of plant D-fucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression; LC-MS; purification and NMR of downstream products; recombinant protein assays in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT1 family, subgroup L ester-forming glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest coexpression with QsbAS1 (PCC 0.987); located in biosynthetic gene cluster 45; high in primordia
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro assays with ATCV-1 UGD and QsFucSyn
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential but bottleneck-forming enzyme for production of C-28 decorated adjuvant saponins; should be paired with QsFucSyn for effective engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initially assigned as fucosyltransferase, but mechanistically acts before SDR-mediated in situ reduction to D-fucose.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose onto the D-fucose residue in the C-28 ester-linked sugar chain.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Builds the linear C-28 tetrasaccharide toward QS-7/QS-21 cores
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; depends on prior fucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the second sugar of the C-28 chain after D-fucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening identified Qs0321920 as a subgroup A UGT adding a second deoxyhexose to putative QA-TriX-F. 2. Product formation depended on presence of D-fucose. 3. NMR of scaled products confirmed it as the C-28 rhamnosyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; product levels improved by QsFucSyn
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for extending the C-28 sugar chain in adjuvant saponin engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located adjacent to UGT74BX1 in biosynthetic gene cluster 45 despite limited amino acid identity (~30%).

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose to the rhamnose-containing C-28 trisaccharide precursor.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the penultimate conserved C-28 sugar chain intermediate before terminal xylose/apiose addition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the third sugar in the linear C-28 chain.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening of subgroup A UGTs identified Qs0234120 as adding a pentose to QA-TriR-FR. 2. Purified product was confirmed by NMR as QA-TriR-FRX. 3. The enzyme therefore encodes the C-28 xylosyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Located in chromosome 7 biosynthetic gene cluster 31
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; product boosted by QsFucSyn
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed to build the complete C-28 tetrasaccharide core in engineered hosts.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose as the terminal sugar of the C-28 tetrasaccharide chain.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the apiosylated heptasaccharide scaffold used for QS-7 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Late branch-determining step; efficiency improved by QsAXS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the terminal apiose required for the core scaffold leading to QS-7.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening identified Qs0234130 as one of two terminal pentosyltransferases. 2. NMR of purified products assigned Qs0234130 as the apiosyltransferase producing QA-TriR-FRXA. 3. Product accumulation increased strongly when QsAXS was coexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression; LC-MS; purification; 1D/2D NMR; donor-supply boosting with QsAXS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Located in chromosome 7 biosynthetic gene cluster 31; ranked 14th by coexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; QsAXS coexpression enhances product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical for producing the apiosylated scaffold required for QS-7 and potentially QS-21-related molecules.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text notes Qs0234130 and Qs0234140 were initially ambiguous until NMR assignment.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose as the terminal sugar of the C-28 tetrasaccharide chain.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the xylosylated alternative heptasaccharide branch of QS scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates the terminal xylosylated alternative branch of the C-28 tetrasaccharide.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening identified Qs0234140 as one of two terminal pentosyltransferases. 2. NMR assigned Qs0234140 as the terminal xylosyltransferase producing QA-TriR-FRXX. 3. Its product accumulated independently of the apiose-boosting effect seen for Qs0234130.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Located in chromosome 7 biosynthetic gene cluster 31; ranked 18th by coexpression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering alternate terminal C-28 sugar composition in designer saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned by NMR after initial ambiguity with Qs0234130.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose onto the advanced C-28 heptasaccharide scaffold as part of QS-7 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Late tailoring step required in the reconstituted QS-7 pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds a glucose residue during final QS-7 assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. During screening for terminal C-28 glycosyltransferases, Qs0321940 showed glycosyltransferase activity giving a product anticipated to be the glucoside of intermediate 18. 2. The gene is in cluster 45 and strongly coexpressed with known QS genes. 3. Inclusion with QsACT1 and Qs0023500 enabled QS-7 production in the full reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression; LC-MS; full-pathway reconstitution with omission logic
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ranked seventh by coexpression with QsbAS1 (PCC 0.956); cluster 45
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required late glycosylation module for QS-7 biosynthesis in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product assignment was based on LC-MS and pathway logic rather than standalone purified NMR product in the provided text.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final sugar decoration on D-fucose branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Final identified glycosylation step for QS-7
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose onto the acetylated late C-28 sugar chain intermediate to complete the final sugar pattern of QS-7.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Final sugar-installing step required to complete QS-7 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for QS-7 branch choice relative to competing glucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the terminal rhamnose required for authentic QS-7 production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening identified Qs0023500 as one of two late UGTs acting on the acetylated substrate. 2. It likely adds L-rhamnose, whereas Qs0213660 adds glucose to the same position. 3. Inclusion of Qs0023500 with Qs0321940 and QsACT1 in the full pathway produced QS-7 matching standard by LC-MS and NMR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression; LC-MS; competition logic with Qs0213660; full-pathway reconstitution; comparison to QS-7 standard; semipure product NMR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Final branch-selecting enzyme for engineering authentic QS-7 rather than alternative glucosylated congeners.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not located in predicted biosynthetic gene clusters; competes with the related UGT73B43 for the same position.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the same late acceptor position competed for by UGT73B44.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with the authentic QS-7 rhamnosylation branch and likely generates alternative natural congeners
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Competes with UGT73B44 for the same late acceptor site, diverting flux away from QS-7.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Qs0213660 was identified as a late UGT likely adding glucose to the acetylated substrate. 2. Coexpression with UGT73B44 did not give products containing both sugars, indicating competition for the same position. 3. The enzyme therefore defines an alternative late branch rather than the authentic QS-7 endpoint.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important to consider or suppress in pathway engineering if authentic QS-7 is desired.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares 72% amino acid identity with UGT73B44.
